articles.title: 194kg of Produce Transferred to Rochdale Food Bank Following Pantry Closure
articles.excerpt: Following the closure of Irene’s Pantry, 194kg of produce was transferred to Rochdale Food Bank by local residents. The community effort was acknowledged at St James.
articles.content_html:
A group of residents transferred 194kg of produce to Rochdale Food Bank following the closure of Irene’s Pantry. The donation was made by Anne, Peter and Walter, who facilitated the transfer.

The food was hosted at St James, where the community effort was appreciated. Those involved in the operation of the Food Bank were thanked for their contributions.

This initiative highlights the ongoing support for local food banks, which play a crucial role in assisting those in need within the community.

The closure of Irene’s Pantry has prompted local residents to step in and ensure that surplus food is redirected to those who require it.

articles.title: Milnrow Football Club Announces New Training Schedule
articles.excerpt: Traffic disruptions have been reported in Milnrow due to an ongoing incident. Drivers are advised to seek alternative routes.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 16 July 2026 at 17:35. 2 source updates have been combined into this article.

Traffic disruptions have been reported in Milnrow due to an ongoing incident. Drivers are advised to seek alternative routes.

Latest update

A transport incident occurred in Milnrow, leading to significant disruptions in the area. Local services have been affected, and passengers are advised to check for updates before travelling.

Details regarding the nature of the incident remain unclear, but it has prompted delays in bus and other transport services. Authorities are currently assessing the situation to restore normal operations.

Passengers may experience longer journey times as services adjust to the ongoing situation. It is advisable to plan ahead and allow extra time for travel.

Earlier developments

Traffic disruptions have been reported in Milnrow following an incident on July 16, 2026. The nature of the incident has not been disclosed, but it is causing delays in the area.

Local authorities are advising drivers to seek alternative routes to avoid congestion. Emergency services are on the scene, and further updates will be provided as the situation develops.

Motorists are urged to remain patient and follow any diversions set up by traffic management teams. The incident is expected to impact travel times throughout the day.

Update timeline

  • 16 July 2026, 17:35: Transport Disruption Reported in Milnrow Following Incident
  • 16 July 2026, 12:23: Traffic Disruption Reported in Milnrow Following Incident

articles.title: Mark Beesley’s Side Secures Victory with Goals Either Side of Half-Time
articles.excerpt: Mark Beesley’s team claimed a win with goals scored before and after the interval. Rochdale AFC was thanked for their visit as they prepare for their return to the Football League this season.
articles.content_html:
A football match saw Mark Beesley’s side secure a victory with goals scored either side of half-time. The match concluded with a positive result for Beesley’s team, demonstrating their strong performance throughout the game.

Rochdale AFC was thanked for their visit, as they continue their preparations for a return to the Football League this season. The match provided valuable experience for both teams as they look to improve their standings.

The game highlighted the competitive spirit of local football, with both sides showcasing their skills. Fans expressed their support for the teams involved, contributing to an engaging atmosphere during the match.

As the season progresses, both teams will aim to build on their performances, with Beesley’s side looking to maintain their momentum and Rochdale AFC focusing on their return to league play.

articles.title: Police Update Following Woman's Death at Higher Tunshill Farm in Milnrow
articles.excerpt: Police have issued a significant update regarding the death of a woman at Higher Tunshill Farm in Milnrow. Investigations are ongoing as authorities seek to establish the circumstances surrounding the incident.
articles.content_html:
A woman was pronounced dead at the scene at Higher Tunshill Farm in Milnrow. Police have confirmed that they are investigating the circumstances of her death.

Authorities have not yet released the woman's identity, pending notification of her next of kin. The investigation is ongoing, and further details are expected to be made public as they become available.

Local residents may notice an increased police presence in the area as officers continue their inquiries. The investigation could lead to disruptions in the vicinity as police conduct their work.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.
